16	
17	`include "defines.svh"
18	
19	import defines::*;
20	
21	//
22	// Instruction pipeline L1 data cache data stage.
23	// - Detects cache miss or hit based on tag information fetched from last
24	//   stage.
25	// - Reads from cache data storage.
26	// - Drives signals to previous stage to update LRU
27	//
28	
29	module dcache_data_stage(
30	    input                                     clk,
31	    input                                     reset,
32	
33	    // To instruction_decode_stage
34	    output local_thread_bitmap_t              dd_load_sync_pending,
35	
36	    // From dcache_tag_stage
37	    input                                     dt_instruction_valid,
38	    input decoded_instruction_t               dt_instruction,
39	    input vector_mask_t                       dt_mask_value,
40	    input local_thread_idx_t                  dt_thread_idx,
41	    input l1d_addr_t                          dt_request_vaddr,
42	    input l1d_addr_t                          dt_request_paddr,
43	    input                                     dt_tlb_hit,
44	    input                                     dt_tlb_present,
45	    input                                     dt_tlb_supervisor,
46	    input                                     dt_tlb_writable,
47	    input vector_t                            dt_store_value,
48	    input subcycle_t                          dt_subcycle,
49	    input                                     dt_valid[`L1D_WAYS],
50	    input l1d_tag_t                           dt_tag[`L1D_WAYS],
51	
52	    // To dcache_tag_stage
53	    output logic                              dd_update_lru_en,
54	    output l1d_way_idx_t                      dd_update_lru_way,
55	
56	    // To io_request_queue
57	    output logic                              dd_io_write_en,
58	    output logic                              dd_io_read_en,
59	    output local_thread_idx_t                 dd_io_thread_idx,
60	    output scalar_t                           dd_io_addr,
61	    output scalar_t                           dd_io_write_value,
62	
63	    // To writeback_stage
64	    output logic                              dd_instruction_valid,
65	    output decoded_instruction_t              dd_instruction,
66	    output vector_mask_t                      dd_lane_mask,
67	    output local_thread_idx_t                 dd_thread_idx,
68	    output l1d_addr_t                         dd_request_vaddr,
69	    output subcycle_t                         dd_subcycle,
70	    output logic                              dd_rollback_en,
71	    output scalar_t                           dd_rollback_pc,
72	    output cache_line_data_t                  dd_load_data,
73	    output logic                              dd_suspend_thread,
74	    output logic                              dd_io_access,
75	    output logic                              dd_trap,
76	    output trap_cause_t                       dd_trap_cause,
77	
78	    // From control registers
79	    input logic                               cr_supervisor_en[`THREADS_PER_CORE],
80	
81	    // To control_registers
82	    // These signals are unregistered
83	    output logic                              dd_creg_write_en,
84	    output logic                              dd_creg_read_en,
85	    output control_register_t                 dd_creg_index,
86	    output scalar_t                           dd_creg_write_val,
87	
88	    // From l1_l2_interface
89	    input                                     l2i_ddata_update_en,
90	    input l1d_way_idx_t                       l2i_ddata_update_way,
91	    input l1d_set_idx_t                       l2i_ddata_update_set,
92	    input cache_line_data_t                   l2i_ddata_update_data,
93	    input [`L1D_WAYS - 1:0]                   l2i_dtag_update_en_oh,
94	    input l1d_set_idx_t                       l2i_dtag_update_set,
95	    input l1d_tag_t                           l2i_dtag_update_tag,
96	
97	     // To l1_l2_interface
98	    output logic                              dd_cache_miss,
99	    output cache_line_index_t                 dd_cache_miss_addr,
100	    output local_thread_idx_t                 dd_cache_miss_thread_idx,
101	    output logic                              dd_cache_miss_sync,
102	    output logic                              dd_store_en,
103	    output logic                              dd_flush_en,
104	    output logic                              dd_membar_en,
105	    output logic                              dd_iinvalidate_en,
106	    output logic                              dd_dinvalidate_en,
107	    output logic[CACHE_LINE_BYTES - 1:0]      dd_store_mask,
108	    output cache_line_index_t                 dd_store_addr,
109	    output cache_line_data_t                  dd_store_data,
110	    output local_thread_idx_t                 dd_store_thread_idx,
111	    output logic                              dd_store_sync,
112	    output cache_line_index_t                 dd_store_bypass_addr,
113	    output local_thread_idx_t                 dd_store_bypass_thread_idx,
114	
115	    // From writeback_stage
116	    input logic                               wb_rollback_en,
117	    input local_thread_idx_t                  wb_rollback_thread_idx,
118	    input pipeline_sel_t                      wb_rollback_pipeline,
119	
120	    // To performance_counters
121	    output logic                              dd_perf_dcache_hit,
122	    output logic                              dd_perf_dcache_miss,
123	    output logic                              dd_perf_dtlb_miss);
124	
125	    logic memory_access_req;
126	    logic cached_access_req;
127	    logic cached_load_req;
128	    logic cached_store_req;
129	    logic creg_access_req;
130	    logic io_access_req;
131	    logic sync_access_req;
132	    logic cache_control_req;
133	    logic tlb_update_req;
134	    logic flush_req;
135	    logic iinvalidate_req;
136	    logic dinvalidate_req;
137	    logic membar_req;
138	    logic addr_in_io_region;
139	    logic unaligned_address;
140	    logic supervisor_fault;
141	    logic alignment_fault;
142	    logic privileged_op_fault;
143	    logic write_fault;
144	    logic tlb_miss;
145	    logic page_fault;
146	    logic any_fault;
147	    vector_mask_t word_store_mask;
148	    logic[3:0] byte_store_mask;
149	    logic[$clog2(CACHE_LINE_WORDS) - 1:0] cache_lane_idx;
150	    cache_line_data_t endian_twiddled_data;
151	    scalar_t lane_store_value;
152	    logic[CACHE_LINE_WORDS - 1:0] cache_lane_mask;
153	    logic[CACHE_LINE_WORDS - 1:0] subcycle_mask;
154	    logic[`L1D_WAYS - 1:0] way_hit_oh;
155	    l1d_way_idx_t way_hit_idx;
156	    logic cache_hit;
157	    scalar_t dcache_request_addr;
158	    logic squash_instruction;
159	    logic cache_near_miss;
160	    logic[$clog2(NUM_VECTOR_LANES) - 1:0] scgath_lane;
161	    logic tlb_read;
162	    logic fault_store_flag;
163	    logic lane_enabled;
164	
165	    // Unlike earlier stages, this commits instruction side effects like stores,
166	    // so it needs to check if there is a rollback (which would be for the
167	    // instruction issued immediately before this one) and avoid updates if so.
168	    // squash_instruction indicates a rollback is requested from the previous
169	    // instruction, but it does not get set when this stage requests a rollback.
170	    assign squash_instruction = wb_rollback_en
171	        && wb_rollback_thread_idx == dt_thread_idx
172	        && wb_rollback_pipeline == PIPE_MEM;
173	    assign scgath_lane = ~dt_subcycle;
174	
175	    // If a scatter/gather is active, need to check if the lane is active.
176	    // This is more than an optimization: if the lane is masked, we need to
177	    // ignore the pointer to not raise a fault if it is invalid.
178	    idx_to_oh #(
179	        .NUM_SIGNALS(CACHE_LINE_WORDS),
180	        .DIRECTION("LSB0")
181	    ) idx_to_oh_subcycle(
182	        .one_hot(subcycle_mask),
183	        .index(dt_subcycle));
184	
185	    assign lane_enabled = !dt_instruction.memory_access
186	        || dt_instruction.memory_access_type != MEM_SCGATH_M
187	        || (dt_mask_value & subcycle_mask) != 0;
188	
189	    // Decode request type. If this instruction is squashed, ignore (same as
190	    // dt_instruction_valid being false). These do not consider if the
191	    // request is legal or possible, just what the instruction is asking to do.
192	    assign addr_in_io_region = dt_request_paddr ==? 32'hffff????;
193	    assign sync_access_req = dt_instruction.memory_access_type == MEM_SYNC;
194	
195	    // Note the last part that checks the store mask. If the store mask is zero
196	    // don't mark this as a memory access request. This is more than an optimization:
197	    // when performing a scatter store,
198	    assign memory_access_req = dt_instruction_valid
199	        && !squash_instruction
200	        && dt_instruction.memory_access
201	        && dt_instruction.memory_access_type != MEM_CONTROL_REG
202	        && lane_enabled;
203	    assign io_access_req = memory_access_req && addr_in_io_region;
204	    assign cached_access_req = memory_access_req && !addr_in_io_region;
205	    assign cached_load_req = cached_access_req && dt_instruction.load;
206	    assign cached_store_req = cached_access_req && !dt_instruction.load;
207	    assign cache_control_req = dt_instruction_valid
208	        && !squash_instruction
209	        && dt_instruction.cache_control;
210	    assign flush_req = cache_control_req
211	        && dt_instruction.cache_control_op == CACHE_DFLUSH
212	        && !addr_in_io_region;
213	    assign iinvalidate_req = cache_control_req
214	        && dt_instruction.cache_control_op == CACHE_IINVALIDATE
215	        && !addr_in_io_region;
216	    assign dinvalidate_req = cache_control_req
217	        && dt_instruction.cache_control_op == CACHE_DINVALIDATE
218	        && !addr_in_io_region;
219	    assign membar_req = cache_control_req
220	        && dt_instruction.cache_control_op == CACHE_MEMBAR;
221	    assign tlb_update_req = cache_control_req
222	        && (dt_instruction.cache_control_op == CACHE_DTLB_INSERT
223	            || dt_instruction.cache_control_op == CACHE_ITLB_INSERT
224	            || dt_instruction.cache_control_op == CACHE_TLB_INVAL
225	            || dt_instruction.cache_control_op == CACHE_TLB_INVAL_ALL);
226	    assign creg_access_req = dt_instruction_valid
227	        && !squash_instruction
228	        && dt_instruction.memory_access
229	        && dt_instruction.memory_access_type == MEM_CONTROL_REG;
230	
231	    // Determine if this instruction accessed the TLB (and thus possibly
232	    // missed it)
233	    always_comb
234	    begin
235	        tlb_read = 0;
236	        if (dt_instruction_valid && !squash_instruction)
237	        begin
238	            if (dt_instruction.memory_access)
239	            begin
240	                tlb_read = dt_instruction.memory_access_type != MEM_CONTROL_REG
241	                    && lane_enabled;
242	            end
243	            else if (dt_instruction.cache_control)
244	            begin
245	                // Only these cache control opertions perform a virtual->physical
246	                // address translation.
247	                tlb_read = dt_instruction.cache_control_op == CACHE_DFLUSH
248	                    || dt_instruction.cache_control_op == CACHE_DINVALIDATE;
249	            end
250	        end
251	    end
252	
253	    assign tlb_miss = tlb_read && !dt_tlb_hit;
254	
255	    // Check for faults. This will set a fault type, and the signal 'any_fault', which
256	    // will cause side effects of subsequent memory operations to be disabled. Each
257	    // of this is only true if the operation is actually requested.
258	    always_comb
259	    begin
260	        unique case (dt_instruction.memory_access_type)
261	            MEM_S, MEM_SX: unaligned_address = dt_request_paddr.offset[0];
262	            MEM_L, MEM_SYNC, MEM_SCGATH, MEM_SCGATH_M: unaligned_address = |dt_request_paddr.offset[1:0];
263	            MEM_BLOCK, MEM_BLOCK_M: unaligned_address = dt_request_paddr.offset != 0;
264	            default: unaligned_address = 0;
265	        endcase
266	    end
267	
268	    assign alignment_fault = (cached_access_req || io_access_req) && unaligned_address;
269	    assign privileged_op_fault = (creg_access_req || tlb_update_req || dinvalidate_req)
270	        && !cr_supervisor_en[dt_thread_idx];
271	    assign page_fault = memory_access_req
272	        && dt_tlb_hit
273	        && !dt_tlb_present;
274	    assign supervisor_fault = memory_access_req
275	        && dt_tlb_hit
276	        && dt_tlb_present
277	        && dt_tlb_supervisor
278	        && !cr_supervisor_en[dt_thread_idx];
279	    assign write_fault = (cached_store_req || (io_access_req && !dt_instruction.load))
280	        && dt_tlb_hit
281	        && dt_tlb_present
282	        && !supervisor_fault
283	        && !dt_tlb_writable;
284	    assign any_fault = alignment_fault || privileged_op_fault || page_fault
285	        || supervisor_fault || write_fault;
286	
287	    // *** Everything below this point needs to check tlb_miss (if applicable)
288	    // and any_fault before enabling operations. ***
289	
290	    // L1 data cache or store buffer access
291	    assign dd_store_en = cached_store_req
292	        && !tlb_miss
293	        && !any_fault;
294	    assign dcache_request_addr = {dt_request_paddr[31:CACHE_LINE_OFFSET_WIDTH],
295	        {CACHE_LINE_OFFSET_WIDTH{1'b0}}};
296	    assign cache_lane_idx = dt_request_paddr.offset[CACHE_LINE_OFFSET_WIDTH - 1:2];
297	    assign dd_store_bypass_addr = dt_request_paddr[31:CACHE_LINE_OFFSET_WIDTH];
298	    assign dd_store_bypass_thread_idx = dt_thread_idx;
299	    assign dd_store_addr = dt_request_paddr[31:CACHE_LINE_OFFSET_WIDTH];
300	    assign dd_store_sync = sync_access_req;
301	    assign dd_store_thread_idx = dt_thread_idx;
302	
303	    // Noncached I/O memory access
304	    assign dd_io_write_en = io_access_req
305	        && !dt_instruction.load
306	        && !tlb_miss
307	        && !any_fault;
308	    assign dd_io_read_en = io_access_req
309	        && dt_instruction.load
310	        && !tlb_miss
311	        && !any_fault;
312	    assign dd_io_write_value = dt_store_value[0];
313	    assign dd_io_thread_idx = dt_thread_idx;
314	    assign dd_io_addr = {16'd0, dt_request_paddr[15:0]};
315	
316	    // Control register access
317	    assign dd_creg_write_en = creg_access_req
318	        && !dt_instruction.load
319	        && !any_fault;
320	    assign dd_creg_read_en = creg_access_req
321	        && dt_instruction.load
322	        && !any_fault;
323	    assign dd_creg_write_val = dt_store_value[0];
324	    assign dd_creg_index = dt_instruction.creg_index;
325	
326	    // Cache control
327	    // Unlike other operations, these check dt_tlb_present, as these will not raise
328	    // a fault if the page is not present.
329	    assign dd_flush_en = flush_req
330	        && dt_tlb_hit
331	        && dt_tlb_present
332	        && !io_access_req // XXX should a cache control of IO address raise exception?
333	        && !any_fault;
334	    assign dd_iinvalidate_en = iinvalidate_req
335	        && dt_tlb_hit
336	        && dt_tlb_present
337	        && !io_access_req
338	        && !any_fault;
339	    assign dd_dinvalidate_en = dinvalidate_req
340	        && dt_tlb_hit
341	        && dt_tlb_present
342	        && !io_access_req
343	        && !any_fault;
344	    assign dd_membar_en = membar_req
345	        && dt_instruction.cache_control_op == CACHE_MEMBAR;
346	
347	    //
348	    // Check for cache hit
349	    //
350	    genvar way_idx;
351	    generate
352	        for (way_idx = 0; way_idx < `L1D_WAYS; way_idx++)
353	        begin : hit_check_gen
354	            assign way_hit_oh[way_idx] = dt_request_paddr.tag == dt_tag[way_idx]
355	                && dt_valid[way_idx];
356	        end
357	    endgenerate
358	
359	    // Treat a synchronized load as a cache miss the first time it occurs, because
360	    // it needs to send it to the L2 cache to register it.
361	    assign cache_hit = |way_hit_oh
362	        && (!sync_access_req || dd_load_sync_pending[dt_thread_idx])
363	        && dt_tlb_hit;
364	
365	    //
366	    // Store alignment
367	    //
368	    idx_to_oh #(
369	        .NUM_SIGNALS(CACHE_LINE_WORDS),
370	        .DIRECTION("LSB0")
371	    ) idx_to_oh_cache_lane(
372	        .one_hot(cache_lane_mask),
373	        .index(cache_lane_idx));
374	
375	    always_comb
376	    begin
377	        word_store_mask = 0;
378	        unique case (dt_instruction.memory_access_type)
379	            MEM_BLOCK, MEM_BLOCK_M:    // Block vector access
380	                word_store_mask = dt_mask_value;
381	
382	            MEM_SCGATH, MEM_SCGATH_M:    // Scatter/Gather access
383	            begin
384	                if ((dt_mask_value & subcycle_mask) != 0)
385	                    word_store_mask = cache_lane_mask;
386	                else
387	                    word_store_mask = 0;
388	            end
389	
390	            default:    // Scalar access
391	                word_store_mask = cache_lane_mask;
392	        endcase
393	    end
394	
395	    // Endian swap vector data
396	    genvar swap_word;
397	    generate
398	        for (swap_word = 0; swap_word < CACHE_LINE_BYTES / 4; swap_word++)
399	        begin : swap_word_gen
400	            assign endian_twiddled_data[swap_word * 32+:8] = dt_store_value[swap_word][24+:8];
401	            assign endian_twiddled_data[swap_word * 32 + 8+:8] = dt_store_value[swap_word][16+:8];
402	            assign endian_twiddled_data[swap_word * 32 + 16+:8] = dt_store_value[swap_word][8+:8];
403	            assign endian_twiddled_data[swap_word * 32 + 24+:8] = dt_store_value[swap_word][0+:8];
404	        end
405	    endgenerate
406	
407	    assign lane_store_value = dt_store_value[scgath_lane];
408	
409	    // byte_store_mask and dd_store_data.
410	    always_comb
411	    begin
412	        unique case (dt_instruction.memory_access_type)
413	            MEM_B, MEM_BX: // Byte
414	            begin
415	                dd_store_data = {CACHE_LINE_WORDS * 4{dt_store_value[0][7:0]}};
416	                case (dt_request_paddr.offset[1:0])
417	                    2'd0: byte_store_mask = 4'b1000;
418	                    2'd1: byte_store_mask = 4'b0100;
419	                    2'd2: byte_store_mask = 4'b0010;
420	                    2'd3: byte_store_mask = 4'b0001;
421	                    default: byte_store_mask = 4'b0000;
422	                endcase
423	            end
424	
425	            MEM_S, MEM_SX: // 16 bits
426	            begin
427	                dd_store_data = {CACHE_LINE_WORDS * 2{dt_store_value[0][7:0], dt_store_value[0][15:8]}};
428	                if (dt_request_paddr.offset[1] == 1'b0)
429	                    byte_store_mask = 4'b1100;
430	                else
431	                    byte_store_mask = 4'b0011;
432	            end
433	
434	            MEM_L, MEM_SYNC: // 32 bits
435	            begin
436	                byte_store_mask = 4'b1111;
437	                dd_store_data = {CACHE_LINE_WORDS{dt_store_value[0][7:0], dt_store_value[0][15:8],
438	                    dt_store_value[0][23:16], dt_store_value[0][31:24]}};
439	            end
440	
441	            MEM_SCGATH, MEM_SCGATH_M:
442	            begin
443	                byte_store_mask = 4'b1111;
444	                dd_store_data = {CACHE_LINE_WORDS{lane_store_value[7:0], lane_store_value[15:8],
445	                    lane_store_value[23:16], lane_store_value[31:24]}};
446	            end
447	
448	            default: // Vector
449	            begin
450	                byte_store_mask = 4'b1111;
451	                dd_store_data = endian_twiddled_data;
452	            end
453	        endcase
454	    end
455	
456	    // Generate store mask signals. word_store_mask corresponds to lanes,
457	    // byte_store_mask corresponds to bytes within a word. byte_store_mask
458	    // always has all bits set if word_store_mask has more than one bit set:
459	    // either select some number of words within the cache line for
460	    // a vector transfer or some bytes within a word for a scalar transfer.
461	    genvar mask_idx;
462	    generate
463	        for (mask_idx = 0; mask_idx < CACHE_LINE_BYTES; mask_idx++)
464	        begin : store_mask_gen
465	            assign dd_store_mask[mask_idx] = word_store_mask[
466	                (CACHE_LINE_BYTES - mask_idx - 1) / 4]
467	                & byte_store_mask[mask_idx & 3];
468	        end
469	    endgenerate
470	
471	    oh_to_idx #(.NUM_SIGNALS(`L1D_WAYS)) encode_hit_way(
472	        .one_hot(way_hit_oh),
473	        .index(way_hit_idx));
474	
475	    sram_1r1w #(
476	        .DATA_WIDTH(CACHE_LINE_BITS),
477	        .SIZE(`L1D_WAYS * `L1D_SETS),
478	        .READ_DURING_WRITE("NEW_DATA")
479	    ) l1d_data(
480	        // Instruction pipeline access.
481	        .read_en(cache_hit && cached_load_req),
482	        .read_addr({way_hit_idx, dt_request_paddr.set_idx}),
483	        .read_data(dd_load_data),
484	
485	        // Update from L2 cache interface
486	        .write_en(l2i_ddata_update_en),
487	        .write_addr({l2i_ddata_update_way, l2i_ddata_update_set}),
488	        .write_data(l2i_ddata_update_data),
489	        .*);
490	
491	    // cache_near_miss indicates a cache miss is occurring in the cycle this is
492	    // filling the same line. If this suspends the thread, it will never
493	    // receive a wakeup. Instead, roll the thread back and let it retry.
494	    // Do not be set for a synchronized load, even if the data is in the L1
495	    // cache: it must do a round trip to the L2 cache to latch the address.
496	    assign cache_near_miss = !cache_hit
497	        && dt_tlb_hit
498	        && cached_load_req
499	        && |l2i_dtag_update_en_oh
500	        && l2i_dtag_update_set == dt_request_paddr.set_idx
501	        && l2i_dtag_update_tag == dt_request_paddr.tag
502	        && !sync_access_req
503	        && !any_fault;
504	
505	    assign dd_cache_miss = cached_load_req
506	        && !cache_hit
507	        && dt_tlb_hit
508	        && !cache_near_miss
509	        && !any_fault;
510	    assign dd_cache_miss_addr = dcache_request_addr[31:CACHE_LINE_OFFSET_WIDTH];
511	    assign dd_cache_miss_thread_idx = dt_thread_idx;
512	    assign dd_cache_miss_sync = sync_access_req;
513	
514	    assign dd_update_lru_en = cache_hit && cached_access_req && !any_fault;
515	    assign dd_update_lru_way = way_hit_idx;
516	
517	    // Always treat the first synchronized load as a cache miss, even if data is
518	    // present. This is to register request with L2 cache. The second request will
519	    // not be a miss if the data is in the cache (there is a window where it could
520	    // be evicted before the thread can fetch it, in which case it will retry.
521	    // load_sync_pending tracks if this is the first or second request.
522	    //
523	    // Interrupts are different than rollbacks because they can occur in the middle
524	    // of a synchronized load/store. Detect these and cancel the operation.
525	    genvar thread_idx;
526	    generate
527	        for (thread_idx = 0; thread_idx < `THREADS_PER_CORE; thread_idx++)
528	        begin : sync_pending_gen
529	            always_ff @(posedge clk, posedge reset)
530	            begin
531	                if (reset)
532	                    dd_load_sync_pending[thread_idx] <= 0;
533	                else if (cached_load_req && sync_access_req && dt_thread_idx == local_thread_idx_t'(thread_idx))
534	                begin
535	                    // Track if this is the first or restarted request.
536	                    dd_load_sync_pending[thread_idx] <= !dd_load_sync_pending[thread_idx];
537	                end
538	            end
539	        end
540	    endgenerate
541	
542	    assign fault_store_flag = dt_instruction.memory_access
543	        && !dt_instruction.load;
544	
545	    always_ff @(posedge clk)
546	    begin
547	        dd_instruction <= dt_instruction;
548	        dd_lane_mask <= dt_mask_value;
549	        dd_thread_idx <= dt_thread_idx;
550	        dd_request_vaddr <= dt_request_vaddr;
551	        dd_subcycle <= dt_subcycle;
552	        dd_rollback_pc <= dt_instruction.pc;
553	        dd_io_access <= io_access_req;
554	
555	        // Check for TLB miss first, since permission bits are not valid if
556	        // there is a TLB miss. The order of the remaining items should match
557	        // that in instruction_decode_stage for consistency.
558	        if (tlb_miss)
559	            dd_trap_cause <= {1'b1, fault_store_flag, TT_TLB_MISS};
560	        else if (page_fault)
561	            dd_trap_cause <= {1'b1, fault_store_flag, TT_PAGE_FAULT};
562	        else if (supervisor_fault)
563	            dd_trap_cause <= {1'b1, fault_store_flag, TT_SUPERVISOR_ACCESS};
564	        else if (alignment_fault)
565	            dd_trap_cause <= {1'b1, fault_store_flag, TT_UNALIGNED_ACCESS};
566	        else if (privileged_op_fault)
567	            dd_trap_cause <= {2'b00, TT_PRIVILEGED_OP};
568	        else // write fault
569	            dd_trap_cause <= {2'b11, TT_ILLEGAL_STORE};
570	    end
571	
572	    always_ff @(posedge clk, posedge reset)
573	    begin
574	        if (reset)
575	        begin
576	            /*AUTORESET*/
577	            // Beginning of autoreset for uninitialized flops
578	            dd_instruction_valid <= '0;
579	            dd_perf_dcache_hit <= '0;
580	            dd_perf_dcache_miss <= '0;
581	            dd_perf_dtlb_miss <= '0;
582	            dd_rollback_en <= '0;
583	            dd_suspend_thread <= '0;
584	            dd_trap <= '0;
585	            // End of automatics
586	        end
587	        else
588	        begin
589	            // Make sure data is not present in more than one way.
590	            assert(!cached_load_req || $onehot0(way_hit_oh));
591	
592	            // Make sure this decodes only one type of instruction
593	            assert($onehot0({cached_load_req, cached_store_req, io_access_req,
594	                flush_req, iinvalidate_req, dinvalidate_req,
595	                membar_req, tlb_update_req, creg_access_req}));
596	
597	            dd_instruction_valid <= dt_instruction_valid
598	                && !squash_instruction;
599	
600	            // Rollback on cache miss
601	            dd_rollback_en <= cached_load_req && !cache_hit && dt_tlb_hit && !any_fault;
602	
603	            // Suspend the thread if there is a cache miss.
604	            // In the near miss case (described above), don't suspend thread.
605	            dd_suspend_thread <= cached_load_req
606	                && dt_tlb_hit
607	                && !cache_hit
608	                && !cache_near_miss
609	                && !any_fault;
610	
611	            dd_trap <= any_fault || tlb_miss;
612	
613	            // Perf events
614	            dd_perf_dcache_hit <= cached_load_req && !any_fault && !tlb_miss
615	                 && cache_hit;
616	            dd_perf_dcache_miss <= cached_load_req && !any_fault && !tlb_miss
617	                && !cache_hit;
618	            dd_perf_dtlb_miss <= tlb_miss;
619	        end
620	    end
621	endmodule

16	
17	`ifndef __CONFIG_V
18	`define __CONFIG_V
19	
20	//
21	// Configurable parameters
22	// - THREADS_PER_CORE must be 2 or greater.
23	// - Number of cache ways must be 1, 2, 4, or 8 (TLB_WAYS does not have
24	//   this constraint). This is a limitation in the cache_lru module.
25	// - L1D_WAYS/L1I_WAYS must be greater than or equal to THREADS_PER_CORE,
26	//   otherwise the system may livelock on a cache miss.
27	// - The number of cache sets must be a power of two.
28	// - If you change the number of L2 ways, you must also modify the
29	//   flush_l2_cache function in testbench/soc_tb.sv. Comments above
30	//   that function describe how and why.
31	// - NUM_CORES must be 1-16. To synthesize more cores, increase the
32	//   width of core_id_t in defines.sv (as above, comments there describe why).
33	// - L1D_SETS sets must be 64 or fewer (page size / cache line size). This
34	//   avoids aliasing in the virtually indexed/physically tagged L1 cache by
35	//   preventing the same physical address from appearing in different cache
36	//   sets (see dcache_tag_stage).
37	// - The size of a cache is sets * ways * cache line size (64 bytes)
38	//
39	
40	`define NUM_CORES 1
41	`define THREADS_PER_CORE 4
42	`define L1D_WAYS 4
43	`define L1D_SETS 64        // 16k
44	`define L1I_WAYS 4
45	`define L1I_SETS 64        // 16k
46	`define L2_WAYS 8
47	`define L2_SETS 256        // 128k
48	`define AXI_DATA_WIDTH 32
49	`define ITLB_ENTRIES 64
50	`define DTLB_ENTRIES 64
51	`define TLB_WAYS 4
52	
53	// Picked random part version and number to have unique pattern to verify.
54	// The manufacturer ID is chosen to be the last possible ID.
55	`define JTAG_PART_VERSION 4
56	`define JTAG_PART_NUMBER 'hd20d
57	`define JTAG_MANUFACTURER_ID {4'b1111,  7'b1111101}
58	
59	`endif
60

content.text: 让我读取相关的 `defines.svh` 部分以及 `store queue`/`L1-L2 interface`，以了解 `store merging` 和 `snoop` 的相关内容。

content.content:
1	//
2	// Copyright 2011-2015 Jeff Bush
3	//
4	// Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
5	// you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
6	// You may obtain a copy of the License at
7	//
8	//     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
9	//
10	// Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
11	// distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
12	// WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13	// See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14	// limitations under the License.
15	//
16	
17	`include "defines.svh"
18	
19	import defines::*;
20	
21	//
22	// Queues store requests from the instruction pipeline, sends store requests to
23	// L2 interconnect, and processes responses. Cache control commands go through
24	// here as well.
25	// A memory barrier request waits until all pending store requests finish.
26	// It acts like a store in terms of rollback logic, but doesn't enqueue
27	// anything.
28	//
29	
30	module l1_store_queue(
31	    input                                  clk,
32	    input                                  reset,
33	
34	    // To instruction_decode_stage
35	    output local_thread_bitmap_t           sq_store_sync_pending,
36	
37	    // From dache_data_stage
38	    input                                  dd_store_en,
39	    input                                  dd_flush_en,
40	    input                                  dd_membar_en,
41	    input                                  dd_iinvalidate_en,
42	    input                                  dd_dinvalidate_en,
43	    input cache_line_index_t               dd_store_addr,
44	    input [CACHE_LINE_BYTES - 1:0]         dd_store_mask,
45	    input cache_line_data_t                dd_store_data,
46	    input                                  dd_store_sync,
47	    input local_thread_idx_t               dd_store_thread_idx,
48	    input cache_line_index_t               dd_store_bypass_addr,
49	    input local_thread_idx_t               dd_store_bypass_thread_idx,
50	
51	    // To writeback_stage
52	    output logic [CACHE_LINE_BYTES - 1:0]  sq_store_bypass_mask,
53	    output cache_line_data_t               sq_store_bypass_data,
54	    output logic                           sq_store_sync_success,
55	
56	    // From l1_l2_interface
57	    input                                  storebuf_dequeue_ack,
58	    input                                  storebuf_l2_response_valid,
59	    input l1_miss_entry_idx_t              storebuf_l2_response_idx,
60	    input                                  storebuf_l2_sync_success,
61	
62	    // To l1_l2_interface
63	    output logic                           sq_dequeue_ready,
64	    output cache_line_index_t              sq_dequeue_addr,
65	    output l1_miss_entry_idx_t             sq_dequeue_idx,
66	    output logic[CACHE_LINE_BYTES - 1:0]   sq_dequeue_mask,
67	    output cache_line_data_t               sq_dequeue_data,
68	    output logic                           sq_dequeue_sync,
69	    output logic                           sq_dequeue_flush,
70	    output logic                           sq_dequeue_iinvalidate,
71	    output logic                           sq_dequeue_dinvalidate,
72	    output logic                           sq_rollback_en,
73	    output local_thread_bitmap_t           sq_wake_bitmap);
74	
75	    struct packed {
76	        logic sync;
77	        logic flush;
78	        logic iinvalidate;
79	        logic dinvalidate;
80	        logic request_sent;
81	        logic response_received;
82	        logic sync_success;
83	        logic thread_waiting;
84	        logic valid;
85	        cache_line_data_t data;
86	        logic[CACHE_LINE_BYTES - 1:0] mask;
87	        cache_line_index_t address;
88	    } pending_stores[`THREADS_PER_CORE];
89	    local_thread_bitmap_t rollback;
90	    local_thread_bitmap_t send_request;
91	    local_thread_idx_t send_grant_idx;
92	    local_thread_bitmap_t send_grant_oh;
93	
94	    rr_arbiter #(.NUM_REQUESTERS(`THREADS_PER_CORE)) request_arbiter(
95	        .request(send_request),
96	        .update_lru(1'b1),
97	        .grant_oh(send_grant_oh),
98	        .*);
99	
100	    oh_to_idx #(.NUM_SIGNALS(`THREADS_PER_CORE)) oh_to_idx_send_grant(
101	        .index(send_grant_idx),
102	        .one_hot(send_grant_oh));
103	
104	    genvar thread_idx;
105	    generate
106	        for (thread_idx = 0; thread_idx < `THREADS_PER_CORE; thread_idx++)
107	        begin : thread_store_buf_gen
108	            logic update_store_entry;
109	            logic can_write_combine;
110	            logic store_requested_this_entry;
111	            logic send_this_cycle;
112	            logic restarted_sync_request;
113	            logic got_response_this_entry;
114	            logic membar_requested_this_entry;
115	            logic enqueue_cache_control;
116	
117	            assign send_request[thread_idx] = pending_stores[thread_idx].valid
118	                && !pending_stores[thread_idx].request_sent;
119	            assign store_requested_this_entry = dd_store_en && dd_store_thread_idx == local_thread_idx_t'(thread_idx);
120	            assign membar_requested_this_entry = dd_membar_en && dd_store_thread_idx == local_thread_idx_t'(thread_idx);
121	            assign send_this_cycle = send_grant_oh[thread_idx] && storebuf_dequeue_ack;
122	            assign can_write_combine = pending_stores[thread_idx].valid
123	                && pending_stores[thread_idx].address == dd_store_addr
124	                && !pending_stores[thread_idx].flush
125	                && !pending_stores[thread_idx].iinvalidate
126	                && !pending_stores[thread_idx].dinvalidate
127	                && !dd_store_sync
128	                && !pending_stores[thread_idx].request_sent
129	                && !send_this_cycle
130	                && !dd_flush_en
131	                && !dd_iinvalidate_en
132	                && !dd_dinvalidate_en;
133	            assign restarted_sync_request = pending_stores[thread_idx].valid
134	                && pending_stores[thread_idx].response_received
135	                && pending_stores[thread_idx].sync;
136	            assign update_store_entry = store_requested_this_entry
137	                && (!pending_stores[thread_idx].valid || can_write_combine || got_response_this_entry)
138	                && !restarted_sync_request;
139	            assign got_response_this_entry = storebuf_l2_response_valid
140	                && storebuf_l2_response_idx == local_thread_idx_t'(thread_idx);
141	            assign sq_wake_bitmap[thread_idx] = got_response_this_entry
142	                && pending_stores[thread_idx].thread_waiting;
143	            assign enqueue_cache_control = dd_store_thread_idx == local_thread_idx_t'(thread_idx)
144	                && (!pending_stores[thread_idx].valid || got_response_this_entry)
145	                && (dd_flush_en || dd_dinvalidate_en || dd_iinvalidate_en);
146	            assign sq_store_sync_pending[thread_idx] = pending_stores[thread_idx].valid
147	                && pending_stores[thread_idx].sync;
148	
149	            always_comb
150	            begin
151	                rollback[thread_idx] = 0;
152	                if (dd_store_thread_idx == local_thread_idx_t'(thread_idx)
153	                     && (dd_flush_en || dd_dinvalidate_en || dd_iinvalidate_en || dd_store_en))
154	                begin
155	                    // Trigger a rollback if the store buffer is full.
156	                    // - On the first synchronized store request, always suspend the thread, even
157	                    //   when there is space in the buffer, because this must wait for a response.
158	                    // - If the store entry is full, but it got a response this cycle,
159	                    //   allow enqueuing a new one. This is simpler, because it avoids
160	                    //   needing to handle the lost wakeup issue (like the near miss case
161	                    //   in the data cache)
162	                    if (dd_store_sync)
163	                        rollback[thread_idx] = !restarted_sync_request;
164	                    else if (pending_stores[thread_idx].valid && !can_write_combine
165	                        && !got_response_this_entry)
166	                        rollback[thread_idx] = 1;
167	                end
168	                else if (membar_requested_this_entry && pending_stores[thread_idx].valid
169	                    && !got_response_this_entry)
170	                    rollback[thread_idx] = 1;
171	            end
172	
173	            always_ff @(posedge clk, posedge reset)
174	            begin
175	                if (reset)
176	                    pending_stores[thread_idx] <= 0;
177	                else
178	                begin
179	                    if ((dd_store_en || dd_flush_en || dd_membar_en
180	                        || dd_iinvalidate_en || dd_dinvalidate_en)
181	                        && dd_store_thread_idx == thread_idx)
182	                    begin
183	                        // Can't issue a new request if the thread is waiting.
184	                        assert(!pending_stores[thread_idx].thread_waiting);
185	                    end
186	
187	                    if (dd_store_en && dd_store_thread_idx == thread_idx
188	                        && pending_stores[thread_idx].sync
189	                        && pending_stores[thread_idx].valid)
190	                    begin
191	                        // A synchronized store is already pending for this thread.
192	                        // This must be the second pass to pick up the result. Ensure
193	                        // some constraints are true:
194	
195	                        // The thread should be unblocked until the L2 cache responds.
196	                        assert(pending_stores[thread_idx].response_received);
197	
198	                        // The restarted store should be synchronized
199	                        assert(dd_store_sync);
200	
201	                        // The request should be for the same address.
202	                        assert(dd_store_addr == pending_stores[thread_idx].address);
203	                    end
204	
205	                    if (send_this_cycle)
206	                        pending_stores[thread_idx].request_sent <= 1;
207	
208	                    if (update_store_entry)
209	                    begin
210	                        assert(!enqueue_cache_control);
211	
212	                        for (int byte_lane = 0; byte_lane < CACHE_LINE_BYTES; byte_lane++)
213	                        begin
214	                            if (dd_store_mask[byte_lane])
215	                                pending_stores[thread_idx].data[byte_lane * 8+:8] <= dd_store_data[byte_lane * 8+:8];
216	                        end
217	
218	                        if (can_write_combine)
219	                            pending_stores[thread_idx].mask <= pending_stores[thread_idx].mask | dd_store_mask;
220	                        else
221	                            pending_stores[thread_idx].mask <= dd_store_mask;
222	                    end
223	
224	                    if (sq_wake_bitmap[thread_idx])
225	                        pending_stores[thread_idx].thread_waiting <= 0;
226	                    else if (rollback[thread_idx])
227	                        pending_stores[thread_idx].thread_waiting <= 1;
228	
229	                    if (store_requested_this_entry)
230	                    begin
231	                        // Attempt to enqueue a new request. This may happen the same cycle
232	                        // an old request is satisfied. In this case, replace the old entry.
233	                        if (restarted_sync_request)
234	                        begin
235	                            // This is the restarted request after a synchronized load/store.
236	                            // Clear the entry.
237	                            assert(pending_stores[thread_idx].response_received);
238	                            assert(!got_response_this_entry);
239	                            assert(!pending_stores[thread_idx].flush);
240	                            assert(!rollback[thread_idx]);
241	                            assert(dd_store_sync);    // Restarted instruction must be synchronized
242	                            assert(!enqueue_cache_control);
243	                            pending_stores[thread_idx].valid <= 0;
244	                        end
245	                        else if (update_store_entry && !can_write_combine)
246	                        begin
247	                            // New store
248	
249	                            // Ensure this entry isn't in use (or that it is being cleared this
250	                            // cycle)
251	                            assert(!pending_stores[thread_idx].valid || got_response_this_entry);
252	
253	                            assert(!enqueue_cache_control);
254	
255	                            pending_stores[thread_idx].valid <= 1;
256	                            pending_stores[thread_idx].address <= dd_store_addr;
257	                            pending_stores[thread_idx].sync <= dd_store_sync;
258	                            pending_stores[thread_idx].flush <= 0;
259	                            pending_stores[thread_idx].iinvalidate <= 0;
260	                            pending_stores[thread_idx].dinvalidate <= 0;
261	                            pending_stores[thread_idx].request_sent <= 0;
262	                            pending_stores[thread_idx].response_received <= 0;
263	                        end
264	                    end
265	                    else if (enqueue_cache_control)
266	                    begin
267	                        assert(!rollback[thread_idx]);
268	
269	                        pending_stores[thread_idx].valid <= 1;
270	                        pending_stores[thread_idx].address <= dd_store_addr;
271	                        pending_stores[thread_idx].sync <= 0;
272	                        pending_stores[thread_idx].flush <= dd_flush_en;
273	                        pending_stores[thread_idx].iinvalidate <= dd_iinvalidate_en;
274	                        pending_stores[thread_idx].dinvalidate <= dd_dinvalidate_en;
275	                        pending_stores[thread_idx].request_sent <= 0;
276	                        pending_stores[thread_idx].response_received <= 0;
277	                    end
278	
279	                    // If this got a response *and* hasn't queued a new one over the top of it in the
280	                    // same cycle, clear it.
281	                    if (got_response_this_entry && (!store_requested_this_entry || !update_store_entry)
282	                        && !enqueue_cache_control)
283	                    begin
284	                        // Ensure a response isn't sent for an entry that hasn't been sent.
285	                        assert(pending_stores[thread_idx].valid);
286	                        assert(pending_stores[thread_idx].request_sent);
287	
288	                        // Ensure a response isn't sent multiple times
289	                        assert(!pending_stores[thread_idx].response_received);
290	
291	                        // When the L2 cache responds to a synchronized memory transaction, the
292	                        // entry is still valid until the thread wakes back up and retrives the
293	                        // result. If it is not synchronized, finish the transaction.
294	                        if (pending_stores[thread_idx].sync)
295	                        begin
296	                            pending_stores[thread_idx].response_received <= 1;
297	                            pending_stores[thread_idx].sync_success <= storebuf_l2_sync_success;
298	                        end
299	                        else
300	                            pending_stores[thread_idx].valid <= 0;
301	                    end
302	                end
303	            end
304	        end
305	    endgenerate
306	
307	    // New request out.
308	    // XXX may want to register this to reduce latency.
309	    assign sq_dequeue_ready = |send_grant_oh;
310	    assign sq_dequeue_idx = send_grant_idx;
311	    assign sq_dequeue_addr = pending_stores[send_grant_idx].address;
312	    assign sq_dequeue_mask = pending_stores[send_grant_idx].mask;
313	    assign sq_dequeue_data = pending_stores[send_grant_idx].data;
314	    assign sq_dequeue_sync = pending_stores[send_grant_idx].sync;
315	    assign sq_dequeue_flush = pending_stores[send_grant_idx].flush;
316	    assign sq_dequeue_iinvalidate = pending_stores[send_grant_idx].iinvalidate;
317	    assign sq_dequeue_dinvalidate = pending_stores[send_grant_idx].dinvalidate;
318	
319	    always_ff @(posedge clk)
320	    begin
321	        sq_store_bypass_data <= pending_stores[dd_store_bypass_thread_idx].data;
322	        if (dd_store_bypass_addr == pending_stores[dd_store_bypass_thread_idx].address
323	            && pending_stores[dd_store_bypass_thread_idx].valid
324	            && !pending_stores[dd_store_bypass_thread_idx].flush
325	            && !pending_stores[dd_store_bypass_thread_idx].iinvalidate
326	            && !pending_stores[dd_store_bypass_thread_idx].dinvalidate)
327	        begin
328	            // There is a store for this address, set mask
329	            sq_store_bypass_mask <= pending_stores[dd_store_bypass_thread_idx].mask;
330	        end
331	        else
332	            sq_store_bypass_mask <= 0;
333	
334	        sq_store_sync_success <= pending_stores[dd_store_thread_idx].sync_success;
335	    end
336	
337	    always_ff @(posedge clk, posedge reset)
338	    begin
339	        if (reset)
340	        begin
341	            /*AUTORESET*/
342	            // Beginning of autoreset for uninitialized flops
343	            sq_rollback_en <= '0;
344	            // End of automatics
345	        end
346	        else
347	        begin
348	            // Only one request can be active per cycle.
349	            assert($onehot0({dd_store_en, dd_flush_en, dd_membar_en, dd_iinvalidate_en,
350	                dd_dinvalidate_en}));
351	
352	            // Check that above is true for dequeued entries
353	            assert(!sq_dequeue_ready || $onehot0({pending_stores[send_grant_idx].flush,
354	                pending_stores[send_grant_idx].dinvalidate, pending_stores[send_grant_idx].iinvalidate,
355	                pending_stores[send_grant_idx].sync}));
356	
357	            // Can't assert wake and sleep signals in same cycle
358	            assert((sq_wake_bitmap & rollback) == 0);
359	
360	            sq_rollback_en <= |rollback;
361	        end
362	    end
363	endmodule
364

content.text: 现在让我阅读 `l1_l2_interface.sv` 以了解 `snoop` 机制，以及 `L2` 如何在 `snoop` 时使 `L1D` 失效。

content.content:
1	//
2	// Copyright 2011-2015 Jeff Bush
3	//
4	// Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
5	// you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
6	// You may obtain a copy of the License at
7	//
8	//     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
9	//
10	// Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
11	// distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
12	// WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13	// See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14	// limitations under the License.
15	//
16	
17	`include "defines.svh"
18	
19	import defines::*;
20	
21	//
22	// This module is in each core and handles communications between
23	// L1 and L2 caches. It hides the L2 protocol from the execution pipeline,
24	// making it easier to experiment with alternate protocols and interconnects.
25	// Additional things this module handles:
26	// - Tracks pending load misses from L1 instruction and data caches
27	//   (l1_load_miss_queue).
28	// - Tracks pending stores from pipeline (l1_store_queue).
29	// - Arbitrates miss sources and sends L2 cache requests.
30	// - Processes L2 responses, updating L1 instruction and data caches.
31	//
32	// This processes L2 responses using a three stage pipeline:
33	// 1. Sends store address from the response to L1D tag memory (which
34	//    has one cycle of latency) to snoop it.
35	// 2. Checks the snoop responses. If the data are in the cache, selects the
36	//    way for update. For load responses, update tag memory.
37	// 3. Update L1D data memory. It must do this a cycle after updating the
38	//    tags to avoid a race condition becausethey are checked in this sequence
39	//    by the instruction pipeline during load accesses.
40	//
41	// l2i_request_valid does not depend combinationally on l2_ready (to avoid a
42	// loop), but the opposite is not true (see l2_cache_arb_stage).
43	//
44	
45	module l1_l2_interface
46	    #(parameter CORE_ID = 0)
47	    (input                                        clk,
48	    input                                         reset,
49	
50	    // From l2_cache
51	    input                                         l2_ready,
52	    input                                         l2_response_valid,
53	    input l2rsp_packet_t                          l2_response,
54	
55	    // To l2_cache
56	    output logic                                  l2i_request_valid,
57	    output l2req_packet_t                         l2i_request,
58	
59	    // To ifetch_tag_stage
60	    output logic                                  l2i_icache_lru_fill_en,
61	    output l1i_set_idx_t                          l2i_icache_lru_fill_set,
62	    output logic[`L1I_WAYS - 1:0]                 l2i_itag_update_en,
63	    output l1i_set_idx_t                          l2i_itag_update_set,
64	    output l1i_tag_t                              l2i_itag_update_tag,
65	    output logic                                  l2i_itag_update_valid,
66	
67	    // To instruction_decode_stage
68	    output local_thread_bitmap_t                  sq_store_sync_pending,
69	
70	    // From ifetch_tag_stage
71	    input l1i_way_idx_t                           ift_fill_lru,
72	
73	    // From ifetch_data_stage
74	    input logic                                   ifd_cache_miss,
75	    input cache_line_index_t                      ifd_cache_miss_paddr,
76	    input local_thread_idx_t                      ifd_cache_miss_thread_idx,
77	
78	    // To ifetch_data_stage
79	    output logic                                  l2i_idata_update_en,
80	    output l1i_way_idx_t                          l2i_idata_update_way,
81	    output l1i_set_idx_t                          l2i_idata_update_set,
82	    output cache_line_data_t                      l2i_idata_update_data,
83	
84	    // To thread_select_stage
85	    output local_thread_bitmap_t                  l2i_dcache_wake_bitmap,
86	    output local_thread_bitmap_t                  l2i_icache_wake_bitmap,
87	
88	    // From dcache_tag_stage
89	    input logic                                   dt_snoop_valid[`L1D_WAYS],
90	    input l1d_tag_t                               dt_snoop_tag[`L1D_WAYS],
91	    input l1d_way_idx_t                           dt_fill_lru,
92	
93	    // To dcache_tag_stage
94	    output logic                                  l2i_snoop_en,
95	    output l1d_set_idx_t                          l2i_snoop_set,
96	    output logic[`L1D_WAYS - 1:0]                 l2i_dtag_update_en_oh,
97	    output l1d_set_idx_t                          l2i_dtag_update_set,
98	    output l1d_tag_t                              l2i_dtag_update_tag,
99	    output logic                                  l2i_dtag_update_valid,
100	    output logic                                  l2i_dcache_lru_fill_en,
101	    output l1d_set_idx_t                          l2i_dcache_lru_fill_set,
102	
103	    // From dcache_data_stage
104	    input                                         dd_cache_miss,
105	    input cache_line_index_t                      dd_cache_miss_addr,
106	    input local_thread_idx_t                      dd_cache_miss_thread_idx,
107	    input                                         dd_cache_miss_sync,
108	    input                                         dd_store_en,
109	    input                                         dd_flush_en,
110	    input                                         dd_membar_en,
111	    input                                         dd_iinvalidate_en,
112	    input                                         dd_dinvalidate_en,
113	    input [CACHE_LINE_BYTES - 1:0]                dd_store_mask,
114	    input cache_line_index_t                      dd_store_addr,
115	    input cache_line_data_t                       dd_store_data,
116	    input local_thread_idx_t                      dd_store_thread_idx,
117	    input                                         dd_store_sync,
118	    input cache_line_index_t                      dd_store_bypass_addr,
119	    input local_thread_idx_t                      dd_store_bypass_thread_idx,
120	
121	    // To dcache_data_stage
122	    output logic                                  l2i_ddata_update_en,
123	    output l1d_way_idx_t                          l2i_ddata_update_way,
124	    output l1d_set_idx_t                          l2i_ddata_update_set,
125	    output cache_line_data_t                      l2i_ddata_update_data,
126	
127	    // To writeback_stage
128	    output logic[CACHE_LINE_BYTES - 1:0]          sq_store_bypass_mask,
129	    output logic                                  sq_store_sync_success,
130	    output cache_line_data_t                      sq_store_bypass_data,
131	    output logic                                  sq_rollback_en,
132	
133	    // To core
134	    output logic                                  l2i_perf_store);
135	
136	    logic[`L1D_WAYS - 1:0] snoop_hit_way_oh;    // Only snoops dcache
137	    l1d_way_idx_t snoop_hit_way_idx;
138	    logic[`L1I_WAYS - 1:0] ifill_way_oh;
139	    logic[`L1D_WAYS - 1:0] dupdate_way_oh;
140	    l1d_way_idx_t dupdate_way_idx;
141	    logic ack_for_me;
142	    logic icache_update_en;
143	    logic dcache_update_en;
144	    logic dcache_l2_response_valid;
145	    l1_miss_entry_idx_t dcache_l2_response_idx;
146	    logic icache_l2_response_valid;
147	    l1_miss_entry_idx_t icache_l2_response_idx;
148	    logic storebuf_l2_response_valid;
149	    l1_miss_entry_idx_t storebuf_l2_response_idx;
150	    local_thread_bitmap_t dcache_miss_wake_bitmap;
151	    logic storebuf_dequeue_ack;
152	    logic icache_dequeue_ready;
153	    logic icache_dequeue_ack;
154	    logic dcache_dequeue_ready;
155	    logic dcache_dequeue_ack;
156	    cache_line_index_t dcache_dequeue_addr;
157	    logic dcache_dequeue_sync;
158	    cache_line_index_t icache_dequeue_addr;
159	    l1_miss_entry_idx_t dcache_dequeue_idx;
160	    l1_miss_entry_idx_t icache_dequeue_idx;
161	    logic response_stage2_valid;
162	    l2rsp_packet_t response_stage2;
163	    l1d_set_idx_t dcache_set_stage1;
164	    l1i_set_idx_t icache_set_stage1;
165	    l1d_set_idx_t dcache_set_stage2;
166	    l1i_set_idx_t icache_set_stage2;
167	    l1d_tag_t dcache_tag_stage2;
168	    l1i_tag_t icache_tag_stage2;
169	    logic storebuf_l2_sync_success;
170	    logic response_iinvalidate;
171	    logic response_dinvalidate;
172	
173	    /*AUTOLOGIC*/
174	    // Beginning of automatic wires (for undeclared instantiated-module outputs)
175	    cache_line_index_t  sq_dequeue_addr;        // From l1_store_queue of l1_store_queue.v
176	    cache_line_data_t   sq_dequeue_data;        // From l1_store_queue of l1_store_queue.v
177	    logic               sq_dequeue_dinvalidate; // From l1_store_queue of l1_store_queue.v
178	    logic               sq_dequeue_flush;       // From l1_store_queue of l1_store_queue.v
179	    l1_miss_entry_idx_t sq_dequeue_idx;         // From l1_store_queue of l1_store_queue.v
180	    logic               sq_dequeue_iinvalidate; // From l1_store_queue of l1_store_queue.v
181	    logic [CACHE_LINE_BYTES-1:0] sq_dequeue_mask;// From l1_store_queue of l1_store_queue.v
182	    logic               sq_dequeue_ready;       // From l1_store_queue of l1_store_queue.v
183	    logic               sq_dequeue_sync;        // From l1_store_queue of l1_store_queue.v
184	    local_thread_bitmap_t sq_wake_bitmap;       // From l1_store_queue of l1_store_queue.v
185	    // End of automatics
186	
187	    l1_store_queue l1_store_queue(.*);
188	
189	    l1_load_miss_queue l1_load_miss_queue_dcache(
190	        // Enqueue requests
191	        .cache_miss(dd_cache_miss),
192	        .cache_miss_addr(dd_cache_miss_addr),
193	        .cache_miss_thread_idx(dd_cache_miss_thread_idx),
194	        .cache_miss_sync(dd_cache_miss_sync),
195	
196	        // Next request
197	        .dequeue_ready(dcache_dequeue_ready),
198	        .dequeue_ack(dcache_dequeue_ack),
199	        .dequeue_addr(dcache_dequeue_addr),
200	        .dequeue_idx(dcache_dequeue_idx),
201	        .dequeue_sync(dcache_dequeue_sync),
202	
203	        // Wake threads when a transaction is complete
204	        .l2_response_valid(dcache_l2_response_valid),
205	        .l2_response_idx(dcache_l2_response_idx),
206	        .wake_bitmap(dcache_miss_wake_bitmap),
207	        .*);
208	
209	    assign l2i_dcache_wake_bitmap = dcache_miss_wake_bitmap | sq_wake_bitmap;
210	
211	    l1_load_miss_queue l1_load_miss_queue_icache(
212	        // Enqueue requests
213	        .cache_miss(ifd_cache_miss),
214	        .cache_miss_addr(ifd_cache_miss_paddr),
215	        .cache_miss_thread_idx(ifd_cache_miss_thread_idx),
216	        .cache_miss_sync('0),
217	
218	        // Next request
219	        .dequeue_ready(icache_dequeue_ready),
220	        .dequeue_ack(icache_dequeue_ack),
221	        .dequeue_addr(icache_dequeue_addr),
222	        .dequeue_idx(icache_dequeue_idx),
223	        .dequeue_sync(),
224	
225	        // Wake threads when a transaction is complete
226	        .l2_response_valid(icache_l2_response_valid),
227	        .l2_response_idx(icache_l2_response_idx),
228	        .wake_bitmap(l2i_icache_wake_bitmap),
229	        .*);
230	
231	    /////////////////////////////////////////////////
232	    // Response pipeline stage 1
233	    /////////////////////////////////////////////////
234	
235	    assign dcache_set_stage1 = l2_response.address[$clog2(`L1D_SETS) - 1:0];
236	    assign icache_set_stage1 = l2_response.address[$clog2(`L1I_SETS) - 1:0];
237	    assign l2i_snoop_en = l2_response_valid && l2_response.cache_type == CT_DCACHE;
238	    assign l2i_snoop_set = dcache_set_stage1;
239	    assign l2i_dcache_lru_fill_en = l2_response_valid && l2_response.cache_type == CT_DCACHE
240	        && l2_response.packet_type == L2RSP_LOAD_ACK && l2_response.core == CORE_ID;
241	    assign l2i_dcache_lru_fill_set = dcache_set_stage1;
242	    assign l2i_icache_lru_fill_en = l2_response_valid && l2_response.cache_type == CT_ICACHE
243	        && l2_response.packet_type == L2RSP_LOAD_ACK && l2_response.core == CORE_ID;
244	    assign l2i_icache_lru_fill_set = icache_set_stage1;
245	
246	    always_ff @(posedge clk, posedge reset)
247	    begin
248	        if (reset)
249	            response_stage2_valid <= 0;
250	        else
251	        begin
252	            // Should not get a wake from miss queue and store queue in the same cycle.
253	            assert((dcache_miss_wake_bitmap & sq_wake_bitmap) == 0);
254	            response_stage2_valid <= l2_response_valid;
255	        end
256	    end
257	
258	    always_ff @(posedge clk)
259	        response_stage2 <= l2_response;
260	
261	    /////////////////////////////////////////////////
262	    // Response pipeline stage 2
263	    /////////////////////////////////////////////////
264	
265	    assign {icache_tag_stage2, icache_set_stage2} = response_stage2.address;
266	    assign {dcache_tag_stage2, dcache_set_stage2} = response_stage2.address;
267	
268	    //
269	    // Check snoop result
270	    //
271	    genvar way_idx;
272	    generate
273	        for (way_idx = 0; way_idx < `L1D_WAYS; way_idx++)
274	        begin : snoop_hit_check_gen
275	            assign snoop_hit_way_oh[way_idx] = dt_snoop_tag[way_idx] == dcache_tag_stage2
276	                && dt_snoop_valid[way_idx];
277	        end
278	    endgenerate
279	
280	    oh_to_idx #(.NUM_SIGNALS(`L1D_WAYS)) convert_snoop_request_pending(
281	        .index(snoop_hit_way_idx),
282	        .one_hot(snoop_hit_way_oh));
283	
284	    //
285	    // Determine fill way. If this data is already in this cache set, update the
286	    // way that has the data. This handles write updates and cache synonyms
287	    // (two virtual addresses point to the same physical address).
288	    //
289	    always_comb
290	    begin
291	        if (|snoop_hit_way_oh)
292	            dupdate_way_idx = snoop_hit_way_idx; // Update existing dcache line
293	        else
294	            dupdate_way_idx = dt_fill_lru;     // Fill new dcache line
295	    end
296	
297	    idx_to_oh #(.NUM_SIGNALS(`L1D_WAYS)) idx_to_oh_dfill_way(
298	        .index(dupdate_way_idx),
299	        .one_hot(dupdate_way_oh));
300	
301	    idx_to_oh #(.NUM_SIGNALS(`L1I_WAYS)) idx_to_oh_ifill_way(
302	        .index(ift_fill_lru),
303	        .one_hot(ifill_way_oh));
304	
305	    assign ack_for_me = response_stage2_valid && response_stage2.core == CORE_ID;
306	
307	    //
308	    // Update data cache tag
309	    //
310	    assign response_dinvalidate = response_stage2.packet_type == L2RSP_DINVALIDATE_ACK;
311	    assign dcache_update_en = (ack_for_me && ((response_stage2.packet_type == L2RSP_LOAD_ACK
312	        && response_stage2.cache_type == CT_DCACHE) || response_stage2.packet_type == L2RSP_STORE_ACK))
313	        || (response_stage2_valid && response_dinvalidate && |snoop_hit_way_oh);
314	    assign l2i_dtag_update_en_oh = dupdate_way_oh & {`L1D_WAYS{dcache_update_en}};
315	    assign l2i_dtag_update_tag = dcache_tag_stage2;
316	    assign l2i_dtag_update_set = dcache_set_stage2;
317	    assign l2i_dtag_update_valid = !response_dinvalidate;
318	
319	    //
320	    // Update instruction cache tag. For a fill, mark the line valid and update the tag.
321	    // For a invalidate, mark all ways for the selected set invalid
322	    //
323	    assign response_iinvalidate = response_stage2_valid
324	        && response_stage2.packet_type == L2RSP_IINVALIDATE_ACK;
325	    assign icache_update_en = (ack_for_me && response_stage2.cache_type == CT_ICACHE)
326	        || response_iinvalidate;
327	    assign l2i_itag_update_en = response_iinvalidate ? {`L1I_WAYS{1'b1}}
328	        : (ifill_way_oh & {`L1I_WAYS{icache_update_en}});
329	    assign l2i_itag_update_tag = icache_tag_stage2;
330	    assign l2i_itag_update_set = icache_set_stage2;
331	    assign l2i_itag_update_valid = !response_iinvalidate;
332	
333	    // Wake up entries that have had their miss satisfied.
334	    assign icache_l2_response_valid = ack_for_me && response_stage2.cache_type == CT_ICACHE;
335	    assign dcache_l2_response_valid = ack_for_me && response_stage2.packet_type == L2RSP_LOAD_ACK
336	        && response_stage2.cache_type == CT_DCACHE;
337	    assign storebuf_l2_response_valid = ack_for_me
338	        && (response_stage2.packet_type == L2RSP_STORE_ACK
339	        || response_stage2.packet_type == L2RSP_FLUSH_ACK
340	        || response_stage2.packet_type == L2RSP_IINVALIDATE_ACK
341	        || response_stage2.packet_type == L2RSP_DINVALIDATE_ACK);
342	    assign dcache_l2_response_idx = response_stage2.id;
343	    assign icache_l2_response_idx = response_stage2.id;
344	    assign storebuf_l2_response_idx = response_stage2.id;
345	    assign storebuf_l2_sync_success = response_stage2.status;
346	
347	    /////////////////////////////////////////////////
348	    // Response pipeline stage 3
349	    /////////////////////////////////////////////////
350	
351	    always_ff @(posedge clk)
352	    begin
353	        l2i_ddata_update_way <= dupdate_way_idx;
354	        l2i_ddata_update_set <= dcache_set_stage2;
355	        l2i_ddata_update_data <= response_stage2.data;
356	        l2i_idata_update_way <= ift_fill_lru;
357	        l2i_idata_update_set <= icache_set_stage2;
358	        l2i_idata_update_data <= response_stage2.data;
359	    end
360	
361	    always_ff @(posedge clk, posedge reset)
362	    begin
363	        if (reset)
364	        begin
365	            /*AUTORESET*/
366	            // Beginning of autoreset for uninitialized flops
367	            l2i_ddata_update_en <= '0;
368	            l2i_idata_update_en <= '0;
369	            // End of automatics
370	        end
371	        else
372	        begin
373	            // Make sure more than one snoop way isn't a hit
374	            assert(!response_stage2_valid || response_stage2.cache_type != CT_DCACHE
375	                || $onehot0(snoop_hit_way_oh));
376	
377	            // Ensure only one dequeue type is set
378	            assert(!sq_dequeue_ready || $onehot0({sq_dequeue_flush, sq_dequeue_iinvalidate,
379	                sq_dequeue_dinvalidate}));
380	
381	            // Can only send one request per cycle
382	            assert($onehot0({dcache_dequeue_ack, icache_dequeue_ack, storebuf_dequeue_ack}));
383	
384	            // These are latched to delay then one cycle from the tag updates
385	            // Update cache line for data cache
386	            l2i_ddata_update_en <= dcache_update_en || (|snoop_hit_way_oh && response_stage2_valid
387	                && response_stage2.packet_type == L2RSP_STORE_ACK);
388	
389	            // Update cache line for instruction cache
390	            l2i_idata_update_en <= icache_update_en;
391	        end
392	    end
393	
394	    /////////////////////////////////////////////////
395	    // Request logic
396	    /////////////////////////////////////////////////
397	
398	    always_comb
399	    begin
400	        l2i_request_valid = 0;
401	        l2i_request = 0;
402	        storebuf_dequeue_ack = 0;
403	        icache_dequeue_ack = 0;
404	        dcache_dequeue_ack = 0;
405	        l2i_perf_store = 0;
406	
407	        l2i_request.core = CORE_ID;
408	
409	        // Assert the request
410	        if (dcache_dequeue_ready)
411	        begin
412	            // Send data cache request packet
413	            l2i_request_valid = 1;
414	            l2i_request.packet_type = dcache_dequeue_sync ? L2REQ_LOAD_SYNC : L2REQ_LOAD;
415	            l2i_request.id = dcache_dequeue_idx;
416	            l2i_request.address = dcache_dequeue_addr;
417	            l2i_request.cache_type = CT_DCACHE;
418	            if (l2_ready)
419	                dcache_dequeue_ack = 1;
420	        end
421	        else if (icache_dequeue_ready)
422	        begin
423	            // Send instruction cache request packet
424	            l2i_request_valid = 1;
425	            l2i_request.packet_type = L2REQ_LOAD;
426	            l2i_request.id = icache_dequeue_idx;
427	            l2i_request.address = icache_dequeue_addr;
428	            l2i_request.cache_type = CT_ICACHE;
429	            if (l2_ready)
430	                icache_dequeue_ack = 1;
431	        end
432	        else if (sq_dequeue_ready)
433	        begin
434	            // Send store request
435	            l2i_request_valid = 1;
436	            if (sq_dequeue_flush)
437	                l2i_request.packet_type = L2REQ_FLUSH;
438	            else if (sq_dequeue_sync)
439	                l2i_request.packet_type = L2REQ_STORE_SYNC;
440	            else if (sq_dequeue_iinvalidate)
441	                l2i_request.packet_type = L2REQ_IINVALIDATE;
442	            else if (sq_dequeue_dinvalidate)
443	                l2i_request.packet_type = L2REQ_DINVALIDATE;
444	            else
445	                l2i_request.packet_type = L2REQ_STORE;
446	
447	            l2i_request.id = sq_dequeue_idx;
448	            l2i_request.address = sq_dequeue_addr;
449	            l2i_request.data = sq_dequeue_data;
450	            l2i_request.store_mask = sq_dequeue_mask;
451	            l2i_request.cache_type = CT_DCACHE;
452	            if (l2_ready)
453	            begin
454	                storebuf_dequeue_ack = 1;
455	                l2i_perf_store = l2i_request.packet_type == L2REQ_STORE;
456	            end
457	        end
458	    end
459	endmodule

**config.svh:**
- `L1D_SETS 64 // 16k` line 43

- The constraint comment lines 33-37: "L1D_SETS sets must be 64 or fewer (page size / cache line size). This avoids aliasing in the virtually indexed/physically tagged L1 cache..."

- dd_cache_miss lines 505-509
- dd_update_lru lines 514-515
- faults lines 258-285
- store data/mask logic lines 375-469
- perf events lines 614-618

Wait, important subtlety. In dcache_data_stage, the hit check uses `dt_request_paddr.tag` and the data read uses `dt_request_paddr.set_idx`. But the tag stage read tags using `request_addr_nxt.set_idx` which is the VIRTUAL address set index. So:

- Tag stage: reads tags from `request_addr_nxt.set_idx` — this is the virtual address set index (request_addr_nxt = of_operand1[lane] + immediate, which is virtual)
- Data stage: compares `dt_request_paddr.tag` (physical tag from TLB) against `dt_tag[way]` (physical tags stored) — and reads data from `dt_request_paddr.set_idx`.

Wait, but the set_idx is the same for virtual and physical? Because of VI/PT: the set index bits fall within the page offset (bits [11:0] for 4KB pages), and the page offset is NOT translated by the TLB. So virtual set_idx == physical set_idx. That's the whole point of VI/PT — the index comes from the untranslated (page-offset) bits, so virtual index == physical index.

Let me verify: l1d_addr_t = {tag, set_idx, offset}. The offset is CACHE_LINE_OFFSET_WIDTH=6 bits (bits [5:0]). set_idx is $clog2(L1D_SETS)=6 bits (bits [11:6]). So set_idx+offset = bits [11:0] = page offset (PAGE_SIZE=4096=2^12, so page offset is bits [11:0]). 

So the set index (bits [11:6]) and offset (bits [5:0]) together = bits [11:0] which is exactly the 12-bit page offset that is NOT translated by the TLB. The TLB only translates the page number (bits [31:12]). So:
- virtual set_idx == physical set_idx (always, by construction)
- virtual offset == physical offset
- The tag is the physical page number

This is why VI/PT works without aliasing AS LONG AS the set index stays within the page offset. That requires L1D_SETS * CACHE_LINE_BYTES <= PAGE_SIZE, i.e., 6 bits set_idx + 6 bits offset = 12 bits <= 12 bits page offset. So 2^6 = 64 sets max.

If L1D_SETS were 128 (7 bits), then set_idx would be bits [12:6], and bit 12 is part of the page number (translated), so the same physical address could map to different sets depending on the virtual address → aliasing.

The comment in dcache_tag_stage.sv lines 33-37 confirms: "the size of a way must be the same size or smaller than a virtual page (cache line size * num sets <= page_size)."

And config.svh lines 33-37 confirm the L1D_SETS <= 64 constraint.

Now for the snoop:
- The tag stage has a second read port on sram_tags (read2) for snooping: read2_en = l2i_snoop_en, read2_addr = l2i_snoop_set, read2_data = dt_snoop_tag[way_idx] (lines 188-190).
- It also reads line_valid for snoop: dt_snoop_valid[way_idx] (lines 222-228).
- l1_l2_interface stage1: l2i_snoop_en = l2_response_valid && l2_response.cache_type == CT_DCACHE (line 237); l2i_snoop_set = dcache_set_stage1 = l2_response.address[$clog2(L1D_SETS)-1:0] (lines 235, 238).
- l1_l2_interface stage2: snoop_hit_way_oh[way] = dt_snoop_tag[way] == dcache_tag_stage2 && dt_snoop_valid[way] (lines 275-276). dcache_tag_stage2 = response_stage2.address tag (line 266).
- This handles write updates (STORE_ACK updates the line) and cache synonyms. dupdate_way_idx = snoop_hit_way_idx if hit else dt_fill_lru (lines 289-295).

The snoop uses physical addresses (the L2 response address is physical). The tag memory is virtually indexed but since set_idx is within page offset, physical set_idx == virtual set_idx, so snoop_set (physical) correctly indexes the tag RAM. The comment lines 33-37 explicitly says this is why the way size constraint exists.

Let me write the math for the aliasing avoidance.

The page offset = log2(PAGE_SIZE) = 12 bits.
Cache line offset = log2(CACHE_LINE_BYTES) = log2(64) = 6 bits.
Set index = log2(L1D_SETS) = 6 bits (for 64 sets).
So index+offset = 6+6 = 12 bits = page offset. 

The constraint: log2(L1D_SETS) + CACHE_LINE_OFFSET_WIDTH <= log2(PAGE_SIZE)
i.e. L1D_SETS * CACHE_LINE_BYTES <= PAGE_SIZE
64 * 64 = 4096 = PAGE_SIZE. ✓

I'll present the address breakdown:
\[ \text{addr}[31:0] = \underbrace{\text{page number}}_{\text{20 bits, TLB translated}} \; \underbrace{\text{set index}}_{\text{6 bits}} \; \underbrace{\text{offset}}_{\text{6 bits}} \]

Wait, let me recompute. With PAGE_SIZE=4096, page offset = 12 bits (bits [11:0]). Page number = bits [31:12] = 20 bits = PAGE_NUM_BITS. ✓ (matches line 294: 32 - 12 = 20).

Within l1d_addr_t: offset = 6 bits [5:0], set_idx = 6 bits [11:6], tag = 20 bits [31:12]. DCACHE_TAG_BITS = 32 - 6 - 6 = 20. ✓

So the set_idx is within the page offset (bits [11:6] ⊂ bits [11:0]). Since the TLB only translates the page number (bits [31:12]), the set_idx bits are identical in virtual and physical addresses. This is what makes VI/PT work.

For the hit check, I'll explain the data stage compares physical tag.

Actually, an important subtlety: in dcache_tag_stage, the tag SRAM is read with read1_addr = request_addr_nxt.set_idx (virtual set idx), but the stored tags ARE physical tags. The comparison in data stage is dt_request_paddr.tag (physical) == dt_tag[way] (physical). Good.

And the data SRAM read uses dt_request_paddr.set_idx — which is the physical address set index. But since virtual set idx == physical set idx (VI/PT), this is the same as the virtual set idx. So it doesn't matter that the data stage uses paddr.set_idx; it's identical to vaddr.set_idx.

"""Run unit tests against individual verilog modules.

This compiles each one separately, then looks for the string 'PASS'
in the output.
"""

import os
import subprocess
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, '..')
import test_harness

DRIVER_PATH = os.path.join(test_harness.WORK_DIR, 'driver.cpp')
DRIVER_SRC = """
#include <iostream>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include "V$MODULE$.h"
#include "verilated.h"
#include "verilated_vpi.h"
#if VM_TRACE
#include <verilated_vcd_c.h>
#endif

- Stores go into the store queue (l1_store_queue, one entry per thread)
- Store queue sends to L2

```
l2i_ddata_update_en <= dcache_update_en || (|snoop_hit_way_oh && response_stage2_valid && response_stage2.packet_type == L2RSP_STORE_ACK);
```
So a STORE_ACK updates the L1D data when the line is present (snoop hit). And the store queue's bypass mechanism (sq_store_bypass) lets a subsequent load to the same address read the pending store data before it goes to L2.

So the L1D is effectively "write-around with store buffering": stores buffer in l1_store_queue, get sent to L2, and the L1D line is updated on STORE_ACK if the line is cached. Plus store-to-load bypass for same-address reads.

This is the "store 合并" (store combining/write-combine): in l1_store_queue, can_write_combine merges consecutive stores to the same cache line into one entry (lines 122-132, 218-219).

- Store path: store queue + write combine + store bypass
- Why L1D_WAYS >= THREADS_PER_CORE (avoid livelock via LRU fill priority)

- snoop physical address → tag stage read2 → compare physical tags
- synonym handling: snoop hit updates existing way, else LRU fill
- DINVALIDATE: invalidate on snoop hit

Let me write it now.

One more check — the math: I want to present cleanly.

PAGE_SIZE = 4096 = 2^12, so page offset = 12 bits.
CACHE_LINE_BYTES = 64 = 2^6, so line offset = 6 bits.
L1D_SETS = 64 = 2^6, so set index = 6 bits.
set_index_bits + line_offset_bits = 6 + 6 = 12 = page_offset_bits. ✓

Constraint for VI/PT no-aliasing:
\[ 2^{\lceil\log_2 \text{L1D\_SETS}\rceil} \times \text{CACHE\_LINE\_BYTES} \le \text{PAGE\_SIZE} \]

The address layout:
\[ \underbrace{\text{va}[31{:}12]}_{\text{tag (20b), 经 TLB 翻译}} \; \underbrace{\text{va}[11{:}6]}_{\text{set index (6b)}} \; \underbrace{\text{va}[5{:}0]}_{\text{offset (6b)}} \]

Since set index + offset = page offset = bits not translated by TLB, va[11:6] == pa[11:6]. So virtual index == physical index. That's the crux.

If L1D_SETS = 128 (7-bit index), index = va[12:6], bit 12 is translated → virtual index != physical index → same physical address could land in 2 different sets → alias.

DCACHE_TAG_BITS = 20 (= page number width). The stored tag is the physical page number, which is what the TLB produces.
